title: Market volatility drives China's crude stockpiling
section_path: Market volatility drives China's crude stockpiling
section_path_text: Market volatility drives China's crude stockpiling
summary:
Main
Uncertainty and lower oil prices prompt aggressive stockpiling by Chinese majors, while sanctioned crude imports hold near record highs. By Emma Li
China's seaborne crude imports continued to climb in April, reaching an estimated 10.9 mbd-the highest monthly total since August 2023. This surge was initiated by concerns over potential supply disruptions amid tightening US sanctions, prompting Chinese refiners to ramp up bookings and bolster onshore inventories.
